item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Neat- I really like this. And it turns out that [SCP-4436] (which I wrote after you wrote this, and without me knowing about this) provides a possible means for what they did to Fabi in the final interrogation note.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was really conflicted on this one, but ultimately, I need to downvote.

As an observer of the Jewish faith, I was fairly excited to see an SCP article that used proper biblical terminology and Jewish themes. I was even more hyped by the conprocs, which is very difficult to pul off. However, from the second half of the article and on, all we really get is cruff. The endings don't like up to the expectations, and the idea isn't done creatively from there.

This is an amazing idea, but I'm not too fond of the execution. My apologies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Enjoyed & it doesn't suffer from the usual problem of Judaeo-Christian scips as being written either to portray the faith as just an other anomaly, or being intentionally unclear in what is actually being presented.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This doesn’t really work for me.
It’s just the rapture (which isn’t even a catholic belief, or at least not as portrayed here), played mostly straight. The Vatican’s magical ninjas are funny, I concede that, but they don’t really build on the central concept. The mentions of the Scarlet King and the other GOIs don’t really add anything, either.
-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is really good and I hate to see the votes this misses because of the ending. It deserves a lot more credit than where it currently sits.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm oddly torn on this.

I don't really dig the whole thaumaturgic genre of SCPs. Throwing straight-up magic, runes, protective wards etc. into the mix has never been my cup of tea.

Similarly, I've never been big on religion-centric SCPs. It always feels a little on-the-nose to me to have SCP objects that just about canonise IRL religions documents & events in-universe.

Despite all of that, you wrote about a straight-up horn from the Battle of Jericho, kept secured with the use of thaumaturgic seals, and I actually kinda liked it. The hell did you do?

One of the main things I liked is that - similarly to Clef's 001 proposal having no instances of the words "Angel" or "Eden" - the word "Jericho" is completely absent here, leaving that specific significance alluded but not outright stated.

I liked how the Foundation had to escalate the interrogations to circumvent the prisoners tricks, though I find the second one egregious in that surely the Foundation ought to have cuffed the guy to a chair? You go as far as to mention "unclear capabilities of the intruders" but the Foundation didn't think to physically restrain them?

Even with cuffed hands you could still have the angle of him yanking his wrist through the cuff to do the same thing while missing the odd logic of having this clearly dangerous individual's hands free.

One more nitpick I have:
First interrogation: "spelleater rounds" sounds silly to me, or at very least not clinical. Instead of "rounds", I think "munitions" would be a more appropriate term.

While I do agree with the criticisms posted above, I'm going to tentatively upvote because I still kind of enjoyed it against all odds.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like this adequately captures the existential horror of an item literally capable of ending existence. But it only does so adequately.

The end of the world is kind of a trope at this point, so I can't bring myself to upvote until I see a more unique spin on it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm a little disappointed that there were no references to the different rhythms of the Shofar blast used in Jewish ritual. That could potentially have been interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ spoiler
– hide block


The additional material and new ending haven't helped the main issue I have with this, which is that all the well- executed buildup doesn't really amount to anything. The final activation just being the rapture is really underwhelming, and having the ending fizzle after reading through such long and dense sections leading up to it is disappointing.

item.title: Reserved for author
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to fieldstone for reading over the original draft and making several suggestions, and to gee0765 for his feedback on my most recent draft prior to posting. Hope you all enjoy!

Image taken from: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Shofar-16-Zachi-Evenor.jpg
Credit to Zachi Evenor for the original photo.
